content: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch encode() and possibly encode_ordinary()/_encode_bytes internals. Let me know if you're touching core.py too.

agent1: done. My change to encode() in tiktoken/core.py: added 'return_positions: bool = False' as the LAST keyword-only param, changed return type to 'list[int] | tuple[list[int], list[int]]'. Body change: the two 'return self._core_bpe.encode(...)' lines now assign to 'tokens' instead of returning directly, then at the end: 'if return_positions: _, positions = self.decode_with_offsets(tokens); return tokens, positions' followed by 'return tokens'. If you add 'transformers' param, put it before return_positions in the signature and apply it to text before the special-token check (top of function body) - should merge cleanly since we touch different regions. Tests pass except pre-existing unrelated failure in test_hyp_roundtrip (present on clean main too).
